NKJV.1: Then He spoke a parable to them, that men always ought to pray and not lose heart,
NKJV.2: saying: “There was in a certain city a judge who did not fear God nor regard man.
NKJV.3: Now there was a widow in that city; and she came to him, saying, ‘Get justice for me from my adversary.’
NKJV.4: And he would not for a while; but afterward he said within himself, ‘Though I do not fear God nor regard man,
NKJV.5: yet because this widow troubles me I will avenge her, lest by her continual coming she weary me.’ ”
NKJV.6: Then the Lord said, “Hear what the unjust judge said.
NKJV.7: And  shall God not avenge His own elect who cry out day and night to Him, though He bears long with them?
NKJV.8: I tell you  that He will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will He really find faith on the earth?”
NKJV.9: Also He spoke this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and despised others:
NKJV.10: “Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
NKJV.11: The Pharisee  stood and prayed thus with himself,  ‘God, I thank You that I am not like other men—extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this tax collector.
NKJV.12: I fast twice a week; I give tithes of all that I possess.’
NKJV.13: And the tax collector, standing afar off, would not so much as raise his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me a sinner!’
NKJV.14: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.”
NKJV.15: Then they also brought infants to Him that He might touch them; but when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
NKJV.16: But Jesus called them to Him and said, “Let the little children come to Me, and do not forbid them; for of such is the kingdom of God.
NKJV.17: Assuredly, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will by no means enter it.”
NKJV.18: Now a certain ruler asked Him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
NKJV.19: So Jesus said to him, “Why do you call Me good? No one is good but One, that is, God.
NKJV.20: You know the commandments:  ‘Do not commit adultery,’ ‘Do not murder,’ ‘Do not steal,’ ‘Do not bear false witness,’  ‘Honor your father and your mother.’ ”
NKJV.21: And he said, “All these things I have kept from my youth.”
NKJV.22: So when Jesus heard these things, He said to him, “You still lack one thing.  Sell all that you have and distribute to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me.”
NKJV.23: But when he heard this, he became very sorrowful, for he was very rich.
NKJV.24: And when Jesus saw that he became very sorrowful, He said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter the kingdom of God!
NKJV.25: For it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”
NKJV.26: And those who heard it said, “Who then can be saved?”
NKJV.27: But He said, “The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.”
NKJV.28: Then Peter said, “See, we have left all and followed You.”
NKJV.29: So He said to them, “Assuredly, I say to you,  there is no one who has left house or parents or brothers or wife or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,
NKJV.30: who shall not receive many times more in this present time, and in the age to come eternal life.”
NKJV.31: Then He took the twelve aside and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all things  that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be accomplished.
NKJV.32: For  He will be delivered to the Gentiles and will be mocked and insulted and spit upon.
NKJV.33: They will scourge Him and kill Him. And the third day He will rise again.”
NKJV.34: But they understood none of these things; this saying was hidden from them, and they did not know the things which were spoken.
NKJV.35: Then it happened, as He was coming near Jericho, that a certain blind man sat by the road begging.
NKJV.36: And hearing a multitude passing by, he asked what it meant.
NKJV.37: So they told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.
NKJV.38: And he cried out, saying, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
NKJV.39: Then those who went before warned him that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
NKJV.40: So Jesus stood still and commanded him to be brought to Him. And when he had come near, He asked him,
NKJV.41: saying, “What do you want Me to do for you?” He said, “Lord, that I may receive my sight.”
NKJV.42: Then Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight;  your faith has made you well.”
NKJV.43: And immediately he received his sight, and followed Him, glorifying God. And all the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God.

ESV.1: And he told them a parable to the effect that they ought always to pray and not lose heart.
ESV.2: He said, “In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected man.
ESV.3: And there was a widow in that city who kept coming to him and saying, ‘Give me justice against my adversary.’
ESV.4: For a while he refused, but afterward he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God nor respect man,
ESV.5: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will give her justice, so that she will not beat me down by her continual coming.’”
ESV.6: And the Lord said, “Hear what the unrighteous judge says.
ESV.7: And will not God give justice to his elect, who cry to him day and night?  Will he delay long over them?
ESV.8: I tell you, he will give justice to them speedily. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?”
ESV.9: He also told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and treated others with contempt:
ESV.10: “Two men went up into the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
ESV.11: The Pharisee, standing by himself, prayed  thus: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like other men, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
ESV.12: I fast twice a week; I give tithes of all that I get.’
ESV.13: But the tax collector, standing far off, would not even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’
ESV.14: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified, rather than the other. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.”
ESV.15: Now they were bringing even infants to him that he might touch them. And when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
ESV.16: But Jesus called them to him, saying,  “Let the children come to me, and do not hinder them, for to such belongs the kingdom of God.
ESV.17: Truly, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child shall not enter it.”
ESV.18: And a ruler asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
ESV.19: And Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.
ESV.20: You know the commandments: ‘Do not commit adultery, Do not murder, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honor your father and mother.’”
ESV.21: And he said, “All these I have kept from my youth.”
ESV.22: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, “One thing you still lack. Sell all that you have and distribute to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.”
ESV.23: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was extremely rich.
ESV.24: Jesus, seeing that he had become sad, said,  “How difficult it is for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!
ESV.25: For it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
ESV.26: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”
ESV.27: But he said,  “What is impossible with man is possible with God.”
ESV.28: And Peter said, “See, we have left our homes and followed you.”
ESV.29: And he said to them, “Truly, I say to you, there is no one who has left house or wife or brothers  or parents or children, for the sake of the kingdom of God,
ESV.30: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the age to come eternal life.”
ESV.31: And taking the twelve, he said to them, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written about the Son of Man by the prophets will be accomplished.
ESV.32: For he will be delivered over to the Gentiles and will be mocked and shamefully treated and spit upon.
ESV.33: And after flogging him, they will kill him, and on the third day he will rise.”
ESV.34: But they understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they did not grasp what was said.
ESV.35: As he drew near to Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.
ESV.36: And hearing a crowd going by, he inquired what this meant.
ESV.37: They told him, “Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.”
ESV.38: And he cried out, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
ESV.39: And those who were in front rebuked him, telling him to be silent. But he cried out all the more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
ESV.40: And Jesus stopped and commanded him to be brought to him. And when he came near, he asked him,
ESV.41: “What do you want me to do for you?” He said, “Lord, let me recover my sight.”
ESV.42: And Jesus said to him, “Recover your sight; your faith has made you well.”
ESV.43: And immediately he recovered his sight and followed him, glorifying God. And all the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God.

NLT.1: One day Jesus told his disciples a story to show that they should always pray and never give up.
NLT.2: “There was a judge in a certain city,” he said, “who neither feared God nor cared about people.
NLT.3: A widow of that city came to him repeatedly, saying, ‘Give me justice in this dispute with my enemy.’
NLT.4: The judge ignored her for a while, but finally he said to himself, ‘I don’t fear God or care about people,
NLT.5: but this woman is driving me crazy. I’m going to see that she gets justice, because she is wearing me out with her constant requests!’”
NLT.6: Then the Lord said, “Learn a lesson from this unjust judge.
NLT.7: Even he rendered a just decision in the end. So don’t you think God will surely give justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he keep putting them off?
NLT.8: I tell you, he will grant justice to them quickly! But when the Son of Man returns, how many will he find on the earth who have faith?”
NLT.9: Then Jesus told this story to some who had great confidence in their own righteousness and scorned everyone else:
NLT.10: “Two men went to the Temple to pray. One was a Pharisee, and the other was a despised tax collector.
NLT.11: The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed this prayer : ‘I thank you, God, that I am not like other people — cheaters, sinners, adulterers. I’m certainly not like that tax collector!
NLT.12: I fast twice a week, and I give you a tenth of my income.’
NLT.13: “But the tax collector stood at a distance and dared not even lift his eyes to heaven as he prayed. Instead, he beat his chest in sorrow, saying, ‘O God, be merciful to me, for I am a sinner.’
NLT.14: I tell you, this sinner, not the Pharisee, returned home justified before God. For those who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted.”
NLT.15: One day some parents brought their little children to Jesus so he could touch and bless them. But when the disciples saw this, they scolded the parents for bothering him.
NLT.16: Then Jesus called for the children and said to the disciples, “Let the children come to me. Don’t stop them! For the Kingdom of God belongs to those who are like these children.
NLT.17: I tell you the truth, anyone who doesn’t receive the Kingdom of God like a child will never enter it.”
NLT.18: Once a religious leader asked Jesus this question: “Good Teacher, what should I do to inherit eternal life?”
NLT.19: “Why do you call me good?” Jesus asked him. “Only God is truly good.
NLT.20: But to answer your question, you know the commandments: ‘You must not commit adultery. You must not murder. You must not steal. You must not testify falsely. Honor your father and mother.’ ”
NLT.21: The man replied, “I’ve obeyed all these commandments since I was young.”
NLT.22: When Jesus heard his answer, he said, “There is still one thing you haven’t done. Sell all your possessions and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
NLT.23: But when the man heard this he became very sad, for he was very rich.
NLT.24: When Jesus saw this, he said, “How hard it is for the rich to enter the Kingdom of God!
NLT.25: In fact, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the Kingdom of God!”
NLT.26: Those who heard this said, “Then who in the world can be saved?”
NLT.27: He replied, “What is impossible for people is possible with God.”
NLT.28: Peter said, “We’ve left our homes to follow you.”
NLT.29: “Yes,” Jesus replied, “and I assure you that everyone who has given up house or wife or brothers or parents or children, for the sake of the Kingdom of God,
NLT.30: will be repaid many times over in this life, and will have eternal life in the world to come.”
NLT.31: Taking the twelve disciples aside, Jesus said, “Listen, we’re going up to Jerusalem, where all the predictions of the prophets concerning the Son of Man will come true.
NLT.32: He will be handed over to the Romans, and he will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit upon.
NLT.33: They will flog him with a whip and kill him, but on the third day he will rise again.”
NLT.34: But they didn’t understand any of this. The significance of his words was hidden from them, and they failed to grasp what he was talking about.
NLT.35: As Jesus approached Jericho, a blind beggar was sitting beside the road.
NLT.36: When he heard the noise of a crowd going past, he asked what was happening.
NLT.37: They told him that Jesus the Nazarene was going by.
NLT.38: So he began shouting, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
NLT.39: “Be quiet!” the people in front yelled at him. But he only shouted louder, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
NLT.40: When Jesus heard him, he stopped and ordered that the man be brought to him. As the man came near, Jesus asked him,
NLT.41: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord,” he said, “I want to see!”
NLT.42: And Jesus said, “All right, receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.”
NLT.43: Instantly the man could see, and he followed Jesus, praising God. And all who saw it praised God, too.

WEB.1: He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray, and not give up,
WEB.2: saying, “There was a judge in a certain city who didn’t fear God, and didn’t respect man.
WEB.3: A widow was in that city, and she often came to him, saying, ‘Defend me from my adversary!’
WEB.4: He wouldn’t for a while, but afterward he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God, nor respect man,
WEB.5: yet because this widow bothers me, I will defend her, or else she will wear me out by her continual coming.’”
WEB.6: The Lord said, “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says.
WEB.7: Won’t God avenge his chosen ones, who are crying out to him day and night, and yet he exercises patience with them?
WEB.8: I tell you that he will avenge them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?”
WEB.9: He spoke also this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others.
WEB.10: “Two men went up into the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.
WEB.11: The Pharisee stood and prayed to himself like this: ‘God, I thank you, that I am not like the rest of men, extortionists, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.
WEB.12: I fast twice a week. I give tithes of all that I get.’
WEB.13: But the tax collector, standing far away, wouldn’t even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’
WEB.14: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.”
WEB.15: They were also bringing their babies to him, that he might touch them. But when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
WEB.16: Jesus summoned them, saying, “Allow the little children to come to me, and don’t hinder them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.
WEB.17: Most certainly, I tell you, whoever doesn’t receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”
WEB.18: A certain ruler asked him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
WEB.19: Jesus asked him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good, except one — God.
WEB.20: You know the commandments: ‘Don’t commit adultery,’ ‘Don’t murder,’ ‘Don’t steal,’ ‘Don’t give false testimony,’ ‘Honor your father and your mother.’”
WEB.21: He said, “I have observed all these things from my youth up.”
WEB.22: When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, “You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have, and distribute it to the poor. You will have treasure in heaven. Come, follow me.”
WEB.23: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was very rich.
WEB.24: Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!
WEB.25: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”
WEB.26: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”
WEB.27: But he said, “The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.”
WEB.28: Peter said, “Look, we have left everything, and followed you.”
WEB.29: He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children, for God’s Kingdom’s sake,
WEB.30: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life.”
WEB.31: He took the twelve aside, and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed.
WEB.32: For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles, will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit on.
WEB.33: They will scourge and kill him. On the third day, he will rise again.”
WEB.34: They understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they didn’t understand the things that were said.
WEB.35: As he came near Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the road, begging.
WEB.36: Hearing a multitude going by, he asked what this meant.
WEB.37: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.
WEB.38: He cried out, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”
WEB.39: Those who led the way rebuked him, that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”
WEB.40: Standing still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to him. When he had come near, he asked him,
WEB.41: “What do you want me to do?” He said, “Lord, that I may see again.”
WEB.42: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you.”
WEB.43: Immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.

AMP.1: Now Jesus was telling the disciples a parable to make the point that at all times they ought to pray and not give up and lose heart,
AMP.2: saying, "In a certain city there was a judge who did not fear God and had no respect for man.
AMP.3: There was a [desperate] widow in that city and she kept coming to him and saying, 'Give me justice and legal protection from my adversary.'
AMP.4: For a time he would not; but later he said to himself, 'Even though I do not fear God nor respect man,
AMP.5: yet because this widow continues to bother me, I will give her justice and legal protection; otherwise by continually coming she [will be an intolerable annoyance and she] will wear me out.'"
AMP.6: Then the Lord said, "Listen to what the unjust judge says!
AMP.7: And will not [our just] God defend and avenge His elect [His chosen ones] who cry out to Him day and night? Will He delay [in providing justice] on their behalf?
AMP.8: I tell you that He will defend and avenge them quickly. However, when the Son of Man comes, will He find [this kind of persistent] faith on the earth?"
AMP.9: He also told this parable to some people who trusted in themselves and were confident that they were righteous [posing outwardly as upright and in right standing with God], and who viewed others with contempt:
AMP.10: "Two men went up into the temple [enclosure] to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
AMP.11: The Pharisee stood [ostentatiously] and began praying to himself [in a self-righteous way, saying]: 'God, I thank You that I am not like the rest of men—swindlers, unjust (dishonest), adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
AMP.12: I fast twice a week; I pay tithes of all that I get.'
AMP.13: But the tax collector, standing at a distance, would not even raise his eyes toward heaven, but was striking his chest [in humility and repentance], saying, 'God, be merciful and gracious to me, the [especially wicked] sinner [that I am]!'
AMP.14: I tell you, this man went to his home justified [forgiven of the guilt of sin and placed in right standing with God] rather than the other man; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself [forsaking self-righteous pride] will be exalted."
AMP.15: Now they were also bringing their babies to Him, so that He would touch and bless them, and when the disciples noticed it, they began reprimanding them.
AMP.16: But Jesus called them to Himself, saying [to the apostles], "Allow the children to come to Me, and do not forbid them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
AMP.17: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God [with faith and humility] like a child will not enter it at all."
AMP.18: A certain ruler asked Him, "Good Teacher [You who are essentially and morally good], what shall I do to inherit eternal life [that is, eternal salvation in the Messiah's kingdom]?"
AMP.19: Jesus said to him, "Why do you call Me good? No one is [essentially and morally] good except God alone.
AMP.20: You know the commandments: '<e>Do not commit adultery, Do not murder, Do not steal, Do not testify falsely, Honor your father and your mother</e>.'"
AMP.21: He replied, "I have kept all these things from my youth."
AMP.22: When Jesus heard this, He said to him, "You still lack one thing; sell everything that you have and distribute the money to the poor, and you will have [abundant] treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me [becoming My disciple, believing and trusting in Me and walking the same path of life that I walk]."
AMP.23: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was extremely rich.
AMP.24: Jesus looked at him and said, "How difficult it is for those who are wealthy to enter the kingdom of God!
AMP.25: For it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man [who places his faith in wealth or status] to enter the kingdom of God."
AMP.26: And those who heard it said, " Then who can be saved?"
AMP.27: But He said, "The things that are impossible with people are possible with God."
AMP.28: Peter said, "Look, we have left all [things—homes, families, businesses] and followed You."
AMP.29: And He said to them, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, there is no one who has left house or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God,
AMP.30: who will not receive many times as much in this present age and in the age to come, eternal life."
AMP.31: Then taking the twelve [disciples] aside, He said to them, "Listen carefully: we are going up to Jerusalem, and all things that have been written through the prophets about the Son of Man will be fulfilled and completed.
AMP.32: He will be betrayed and handed over to the Gentiles (Roman authorities), and will be mocked and ridiculed and insulted and abused and spit on,
AMP.33: and after they have scourged Him, they will kill Him; and on the third day He will rise [from the dead]."
AMP.34: But the disciples understood none of these things [about the approaching death and resurrection of Jesus]. This statement was hidden from them, and they did not grasp the [meaning of the] things that were said [by Jesus].
AMP.35: As He was approaching Jericho [on His way to Jerusalem], it happened that a blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
AMP.36: Now when he heard a crowd going by, he began to ask what this was [about].
AMP.37: They told him, "Jesus of Nazareth is passing by."
AMP.38: So he shouted out, saying, "Jesus, Son of David (Messiah), have mercy on me!"
AMP.39: Those who were leading the way were sternly telling him to keep quiet; but he screamed all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
AMP.40: Then Jesus stopped and ordered that the blind man be led to Him; and when he came near, Jesus asked him,
AMP.41: "What do you want Me to do for you?" He said, "Lord, let me regain my sight!"
AMP.42: Jesus said to him, "Regain your sight; your [personal trust and confident] faith [in Me] has made you well."
AMP.43: Immediately he regained his sight and began following Jesus, glorifying and praising and honoring God. And all the people, when they saw it, praised God.

MSG.1: Jesus told them a story showing that it was necessary for them to pray consistently and never quit.
MSG.2: He said, "There was once a judge in some city who never gave God a thought and cared nothing for people.
MSG.3: A widow in that city kept after him: 'My rights are being violated. Protect me!'
MSG.4: "He never gave her the time of day. But after this went on and on he said to himself, 'I care nothing what God thinks, even less what people think.
MSG.5: But because this widow won't quit badgering me, I'd better do something and see that she gets justice—otherwise I'm going to end up beaten black and blue by her pounding.'"
MSG.6: Then the Master said, "Do you hear what that judge, corrupt as he is, is saying?
MSG.7: So what makes you think God won't step in and work justice for his chosen people, who continue to cry out for help? Won't he stick up for them?
MSG.8: I assure you, he will. He will not drag his feet. But how much of that kind of persistent faith will the Son of Man find on the earth when he returns?"
MSG.9: He told his next story to some who were complacently pleased with themselves over their moral performance and looked down their noses at the common people:
MSG.10: "Two men went up to the Temple to pray, one a Pharisee, the other a tax man.
MSG.11: The Pharisee posed and prayed like this: 'Oh, God, I thank you that I am not like other people—robbers, crooks, adulterers, or, heaven forbid, like this tax man.
MSG.12: I fast twice a week and tithe on all my income.'
MSG.13: "Meanwhile the tax man, slumped in the shadows, his face in his hands, not daring to look up, said, 'God, give mercy. Forgive me, a sinner.'"
MSG.14: Jesus commented, "This tax man, not the other, went home made right with God. If you walk around with your nose in the air, you're going to end up flat on your face, but if you're content to be simply yourself, you will become more than yourself."
MSG.15: People brought babies to Jesus, hoping he might touch them. When the disciples saw it, they shooed them off.
MSG.16: Jesus called them back. "Let these children alone. Don't get between them and me. These children are the kingdom's pride and joy.
MSG.17: Mark this: Unless you accept God's kingdom in the simplicity of a child, you'll never get in."
MSG.18: One day one of the local officials asked him, "Good Teacher, what must I do to deserve eternal life?"
MSG.19: Jesus said, "Why are you calling me good? No one is good—only God.
MSG.20: You know the commandments, don't you? No illicit sex, no killing, no stealing, no lying, honor your father and mother."
MSG.21: He said, "I've kept them all for as long as I can remember."
MSG.22: When Jesus heard that, he said, "Then there's only one thing left to do: Sell everything you own and give it away to the poor. You will have riches in heaven. Then come, follow me."
MSG.23: This was the last thing the official expected to hear. He was very rich and became terribly sad. He was holding on tight to a lot of things and not about to let them go.
MSG.24: Seeing his reaction, Jesus said, "Do you have any idea how difficult it is for people who have it all to enter God's kingdom?
MSG.25: I'd say it's easier to thread a camel through a needle's eye than get a rich person into God's kingdom."
MSG.26: "Then who has any chance at all?" the others asked.
MSG.27: "No chance at all," Jesus said, "if you think you can pull it off by yourself. Every chance in the world if you trust God to do it."
MSG.28: Peter tried to regain some initiative: "We left everything we owned and followed you, didn't we?"
MSG.29: "Yes," said Jesus, "and you won't regret it. No one who has sacrificed home, spouse, brothers and sisters, parents, children—whatever—
MSG.30: will lose out. It will all come back multiplied many times over in your lifetime. And then the bonus of eternal life!"
MSG.31: Then Jesus took the Twelve off to the side and said, "Listen carefully. We're on our way up to Jerusalem. Everything written in the Prophets about the Son of Man will take place.
MSG.32: He will be handed over to the Romans, jeered at, made sport of, and spit on. Then, after giving him the third degree, they will kill him.
MSG.33: In three days he will rise, alive."
MSG.34: But they didn't get it, could make neither heads nor tails of what he was talking about.
MSG.35: He came to the outskirts of Jericho. A blind man was sitting beside the road asking for handouts.
MSG.36: When he heard the rustle of the crowd, he asked what was going on.
MSG.37: They told him, "Jesus the Nazarene is going by."
MSG.38: He yelled, "Jesus! Son of David! Mercy, have mercy on me!"
MSG.39: Those ahead of Jesus told the man to shut up, but he only yelled all the louder, "Son of David! Mercy, have mercy on me!"
MSG.40: Jesus stopped and ordered him to be brought over. When he had come near, Jesus asked,
MSG.41: "What do you want from me?" He said, "Master, I want to see again."
MSG.42: Jesus said, "Go ahead—see again! Your faith has saved and healed you!"
MSG.43: The healing was instant: He looked up, seeing—and then followed Jesus, glorifying God. Everyone in the street joined in, shouting praise to God.

NIV.1:
The Parable of the Persistent Widow
Then Jesus told his disciples a parable to show them that they should always pray and not give up.
NIV.2: He said: “In a certain town there was a judge who neither feared God nor cared about men.
NIV.3: And there was a widow in that town who kept coming to him with the plea, ‘Grant me justice against my adversary.’
NIV.4: “For some time he refused. But finally he said to himself, ‘Even though I don’t fear God or care about men,
NIV.5: yet because this widow keeps bothering me, I will see that she gets justice, so that she won’t eventually wear me out with her coming!’”
NIV.6: And the Lord said, “Listen to what the unjust judge says.
NIV.7: And will not God bring about justice for his chosen ones, who cry out to him day and night? Will he keep putting them off?
NIV.8: I tell you, he will see that they get justice, and quickly. However, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?”
NIV.9:
The Parable of the Pharisee and the Tax Collector
To some who were confident of their own righteousness and looked down on everybody else, Jesus told this parable:
NIV.10: “Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
NIV.11: The Pharisee stood up and prayed about himself: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like other men—robbers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
NIV.12: I fast twice a week and give a tenth of all I get.’
NIV.13: “But the tax collector stood at a distance. He would not even look up to heaven, but beat his breast and said, ‘God, have mercy on me, a sinner.’
NIV.14: “I tell you that this man, rather than the other, went home justified before God. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.”
NIV.15:
The Little Children and Jesus
People were also bringing babies to Jesus to have him touch them. When the disciples saw this, they rebuked them.
NIV.16: But Jesus called the children to him and said, “Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
NIV.17: I tell you the truth, anyone who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.”
NIV.18:
The Rich Ruler
A certain ruler asked him, “Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
NIV.19: “Why do you call me good?” Jesus answered. “No one is good—except God alone.
NIV.20: You know the commandments: ‘Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not give false testimony, honor your father and mother.’”
NIV.21: “All these I have kept since I was a boy,” he said.
NIV.22: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, “You still lack one thing. Sell everything you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
NIV.23: When he heard this, he became very sad, because he was a man of great wealth.
NIV.24: Jesus looked at him and said, “How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!
NIV.25: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”
NIV.26: Those who heard this asked, “Who then can be saved?”
NIV.27: Jesus replied, “What is impossible with men is possible with God.”
NIV.28: Peter said to him, “We have left all we had to follow you!”
NIV.29: “I tell you the truth,” Jesus said to them, “no one who has left home or wife or brothers or parents or children for the sake of the kingdom of God
NIV.30: will fail to receive many times as much in this age and, in the age to come, eternal life.”
NIV.31:
Jesus Again Predicts His Death
Jesus took the Twelve aside and told them, “We are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that is written by the prophets about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
NIV.32: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. They will mock him, insult him, spit on him, flog him and kill him.
NIV.33: On the third day he will rise again.”
NIV.34: The disciples did not understand any of this. Its meaning was hidden from them, and they did not know what he was talking about.
NIV.35:
A Blind Beggar Receives His Sight
As Jesus approached Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside begging.
NIV.36: When he heard the crowd going by, he asked what was happening.
NIV.37: They told him, “Jesus of Nazareth is passing by.”
NIV.38: He called out, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
NIV.39: Those who led the way rebuked him and told him to be quiet, but he shouted all the more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
NIV.40: Jesus stopped and ordered the man to be brought to him. When he came near, Jesus asked him,
NIV.41:
“What do you want me to do for you?”
“Lord, I want to see,” he replied.
NIV.42: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight; your faith has healed you.”
NIV.43: Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they also praised God.
